evidences.title: Saudi Pro League title race: AI predicts outcome and it could be bad news for Cristiano Ronaldo | The National
evidences.content: Andy Lewis April 02, 2026 Add on Google Al Nassr lead on 67 points, with Al Hilal on 64, Al Ahli on 62 and Al Qadsiah on 60, leaving a four-way title race. Cristiano Ronaldo’s side face the toughest run-in, including Al Ahli, Al Qadsiah, Al Hilal and Al Shabab, with the forward returning from a hamstring injury. Simone Inzaghi’s team remain unbeaten with 19 wins and seven draws, and are tipped to finish strongest due to greater consistency. Matthias Jaissle’s Asian champions stay in contention with a favourable run-in but likely need near-flawless form after recent setbacks. “Look away now, Ronaldo,” says the AI prediction, tipping Al Hilal to retain the title while prolonging Al Nassr’s wait since 2019. [...] With eight games to go, and everything to play for, we asked AI to predict the remainder of the 2025/26 season. Below are the results. ## Al Nassr Al Nassr have enjoyed a strong campaign with new signings Kingsley Coman and Joao Felix forming a stellar forward line alongside Ronaldo and Sadio Mane. They stormed out in front with a record 10-game winning start to the season but had a wobble around Christmas and let their rivals back in. To their credit, they have recovered...

evidences.title: Date with destiny: 3 games, 9 days to decide Al Nassr title dream?
evidences.content: Victory for Al Hilal would put the heat back on to Al Nassr ahead of three decisive fixtures - trips to Al Qadsiah and Al Shabab follow Al Ahli - across the next week that will shape the outcome of this season’s title race. A fourth fixture, against Al Hilal in the penultimate round on May 12, could be the most colossal of the lot. Or, in fact, the title could be already decided by then. For sure, the campaign’s second Capital Derby looms large on the horizon. From Al Hilal to now Al Nassr, Jorge Jesus knows how to mastermind a title-winning team From Al Hilal to now Al Nassr, Jorge Jesus knows how to mastermind a title-winning team [...] Fresh in Al Nassr’s mind will be this run of corresponding fixtures from the beginning of the year, in which they slipped up, losing to all of Al Ahli, Al Qadsiah and Al Hilal. In turn, they ceded control of the championship. Having regained their place at the summit thanks to that remarkable RSL run, a similar sequence of results would dent Al Nassr again - and with precious little time to turn it back around for a second time. However, they really cannot afford to look any further ahead than Wednesday’s mouth-watering match-up against the rec...

(Reuters Photo) The Saudi Pro League title race has been thrown wide open after a shock setback for Al-Nassr, turning what looked like a controlled march to the championship into a tense final stretch of the 2025-26 season. evidences.title: A crazy turn of events hands Al-Nassr the Saudi Pro League title ...
evidences.content: This means Al-Nassr can clinch the Saudi League title when they face Al-Hilal on 7 May at Al-Awal Park in the 32nd round of the Roshen League. Before that match, Al-Nassr will play four Roshen League matches against Al-Akhdoud, Al-Ittifaq, Al-Ahli and Al-Qadsia in rounds 28 to 31. Read also: Video: Second in 40 days... Al-Taawoun do Al-Nassr a favour and bring Al-Hilal down with another draw Should Al-Nassr win those matches, the gap with Al-Hilal, currently in second place, will remain at 5 points ahead of their Riyadh derby, provided that ‘The Leader’ also manages to win all their matches. [...] And if Al-Nassr manage to beat Al-Hilal in the derby, the gap will increase to 8 points, with just two rounds remaining until the end of the season, meaning Al-Nassr will be crowned champions of the Roshen League. This would be Al-Nassr’s first Roshen League title in seven years, having last won it in 2019. ### ENJOYED THIS STORY? evidences.title: Cristiano Ronaldo and Al-Nassr’s collapse puts Saudi Pro League title at risk | beIN SPORTS
evidences.content: But in football, everything can change in an instant. Football Report: Al-Qadsiah 3-1 Al-Nassr ### The defeat that changed everything The 3-1 loss to Al-Qadsiah was more than just an upset—it was a turning point. The result not only ended Al-Nassr’s dominant run but also brought Al-Hilal back into the race, tightening the standings and putting the title back in play. What once felt secure is now under threat. ### A title-deciding showdown on the horizon With the season nearing its end, one date now stands out above the rest: May 12. That’s when Al-Nassr and Al-Hilal are set to face each other in what could effectively become a title-deciding clash. evidences.content: If current trends hold, the gap between the two sides could shrink to just two points, making that match a potential final for the championship. ### Cristiano at the center of the pressure In this new scenario, the spotlight falls squarely on Cristiano Ronaldo. The Portuguese star continues his pursuit of the historic 1,000-goal milestone, but beyond personal achievements, there is growing urgency to secure a major team title in Saudi Arabia. Despite his goal-scoring impact, silverware has remained elusive during his time with Al-Nassr. evidences.title: How Cristiano Ronaldo's Al Nassr Can Clinch Saudi Pro League ...
evidences.content: The Portuguese phenom has been chasing a league title with Al Nassr since he made the move from Manchester United in December 2022, but for all his goalscoring efforts, he could not lead his team to silverware. The club finished as the runners-up in his first two campaigns, and then settled for third place last season. It might’ve taken longer than expected, but Al Nassr are now in pole position to win the Saudi Pro League for the first time since signing Ronaldo. With only two games remaining in 2025–26, they sit atop the table with 82 points, five clear of second place Al Hilal. The two powerhouses are set to face off on Tuesday in what promises to be a title-deciding contest, one that could end with tears for Ronaldo either way. ## How Al Nassr Can Finally Win Saudi Pro League [...] Cristiano Ronaldo is inching toward his first trophy with Al Nassr. | Mohammed Saad/Anadolu/Getty Images # How Cristiano Ronaldo’s Al Nassr Can Clinch Saudi Pro League Title vs. Al Hilal The Riyadh Derby could decide the Saudi Pro League title race.
